findings.problem: The artifact set does not freeze one consistent malformed-or-unsupported inbound payload behavior. The task list and design require those payloads to be rejected with a reason string, but the sidecar spec still allows the implementation to either ignore or reject them. That leaves host-visible failure semantics and parser responsibilities implementation-defined for the same first-release command surface.
findings.evidence: /home/madejuele/projects/2K0300/openspec/changes/add-bidirectional-assistant-speed-tuning/tasks.md:32-34 requires malformed or unsupported payloads to be rejected with a reason string; /home/madejuele/projects/2K0300/openspec/changes/add-bidirectional-assistant-speed-tuning/design.md:120-123 repeats the same rejection rule; /home/madejuele/projects/2K0300/openspec/changes/add-bidirectional-assistant-speed-tuning/specs/assistant-telemetry-sidecar/spec.md:11-14 still says the sidecar SHALL ignore or reject malformed and unsupported payloads; /home/madejuele/projects/2K0300/openspec/changes/add-bidirectional-assistant-speed-tuning/specs/runtime-speed-tuning-surface/spec.md:61-83 and :199-206 define ACK/state semantics only for accepted decoded commands and clear events, not for malformed payloads that may lack a valid command frame or seq.
findings.recommendation: Replace the permissive 'ignore or reject' wording with one normative malformed/unsupported-payload contract and specify where the reason surfaces when normal ACK correlation is impossible, so the board-side parser, host workflow, and verification evidence all target the same failure behavior.

findings.problem: The accepted tuning run lifecycle is not frozen to one sequence. The tasks require `enable_tuning_mode -> start -> set_target_speed steps -> stop -> disable_tuning_mode`, but the design also presents `enable_tuning_mode -> set_target_speed -> disable_tuning_mode` as an accepted end-to-end sequence, while the runtime spec clears the full tuning snapshot on an undefined 'accepted stop-to-normal flow'. That leaves it unclear whether `start` is mandatory for a compliant tuning run, whether `stop` alone clears tuning state, whether `disable_tuning_mode` remains required after `stop`, and how many `snapshot_cleared` events the minimal workflow should produce.
findings.evidence: /home/madejuele/projects/2K0300/openspec/changes/add-bidirectional-assistant-speed-tuning/tasks.md:47-50 requires evidence for the minimal sequence `enable_tuning_mode -> start -> set_target_speed steps -> stop -> disable_tuning_mode`; /home/madejuele/projects/2K0300/openspec/changes/add-bidirectional-assistant-speed-tuning/specs/runtime-speed-tuning-surface/spec.md:78-83 and :145-156 say `snapshot_cleared` covers disconnect, explicit disable, or an accepted stop-to-normal flow, but never define that stop-to-normal flow; /home/madejuele/projects/2K0300/openspec/changes/add-bidirectional-assistant-speed-tuning/specs/runtime-speed-tuning-surface/spec.md:192-195 says the end-to-end workflow needs disablement or an equivalent documented return-to-normal action after the run; /home/madejuele/projects/2K0300/openspec/changes/add-bidirectional-assistant-speed-tuning/design.md:160-178 says disconnect, stop, or tuning-mode exit can clear tuning state, and /home/madejuele/projects/2K0300/openspec/changes/add-bidirectional-assistant-speed-tuning/design.md:449-452 calls `enable_tuning_mode`, `set_target_speed`, `disable_tuning_mode` an accepted end-to-end tuning sequence.
findings.recommendation: Freeze one normative tuning-run sequence and define the exact trigger for full-snapshot clearing: whether it occurs on `stop`, on the lifecycle returning to `DISARMED`, only on `disable_tuning_mode`, or on a separately named combined return-to-normal action. The specs and tasks should then require the same ACK/state evidence and `snapshot_cleared` count for that single accepted flow.
